meaning: n.公共浴室,澡堂,<美>(海滨)更衣室
examples: After working in a bathhouse for about 50 days, he decided to go back to school.
For personal hygiene, Wang and her family go to a public bathhouse in the neighborhood.
The mosaic floor of what is believed to have been a Roman bathhouse was also found.
People have dug there in the last 100 years and found a Roman bathhouse.
There is a freestanding pub with billiards, a library, even a traditional Japanese bathhouse.
